lines: India versus Pakistan. Nuclear balance only. Who has the edge?
Start with total estimated warheads. India is around 190. Pakistan about 170. That is close.
Land based missiles matter next. Both have multiple short and medium range systems ready for deterrence.
Sea based deterrence is where India gains a strategic advantage with a growing submarine leg.
Pakistan still relies heavily on rapid-response land systems and tactical nuclear signaling.
So in total warheads, the gap is small. In survivable second strike capability, India looks stronger.
But the main story is not domination. The real story is that both sides can inflict catastrophic damage.
That means the nuclear balance stays extremely dangerous, even if one side has a slight structural edge.
So by pure deterrence math, India may have the broader posture. But this is the closest rivalry in the series.

seo.title: India vs Pakistan — Nuclear Balance Explained ☢️
seo.description:
India vs Pakistan nuclear balance: estimated warheads, deterrence posture, sea-based capability, and second-strike depth.
